text:
Market today: Iran-deal headlines are giving risk assets a Monday morning bid. Lower oil pressure + reduced geopolitical fear = tailwind for tech and growth names, which is your whole book. Don't get too comfortable though — macro relief rallies can fade fast, especially on margin.

Your holdings: AVGO (your largest, down -14.66%) is flagged TRIM — that's real pain amplified by margin, and it's the one that can hurt you most if it keeps sliding toward your $336 stop. PLTR is down -11.08% and flagged SELL — two losers of this size on margin is a capital preservation problem, not just a paper loss. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history — treat it like it can cut in half overnight; margin makes that scenario ugly.

Watch: (1) Whether today's Iran/oil relief rally holds into the afternoon or fades — growth names will follow that tape. (2) AVGO's price action relative to $336 stop; a close below that level is your line in the sand.

Bottom line: Your two biggest losers are on margin — AVGO and PLTR need decisions, not hope.
